name: chrome-release-verify
tagline: End-to-end Chrome CVE backport for an Electron release branch: finds missing fixes, writes/validates cherry-pick patches, opens a PR.
verdict: Rigorous, well-gated backport pipeline: local-first validation (sync/lint) before any push, explicit IN-TREE re-run checks, and deliberate PR-body redaction to avoid tipping off attackers before release. Minor concerns: one scoped git commit.gpgsign=false during conflict resolution, no upfront gh/e-tool check, and no version signal.

highlights.summary: Line 75: 'git add <files> && git -c commit.gpgsign=false am --continue' disables commit signing, scoped to one conflict-resolution step, not global.
The pipeline invokes gh, the internal `e` tool, and node script/lint.js across many steps with no upfront auth/availability check before the multi-step run begins.
No version field and no changelog/dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
